yaml.title: Evaluation
yaml.description: How to measure OMR transcription accuracy

markdown.markdown:
We use alignment-aware sequence matching (`SequenceMatcher`) to compare
predicted and reference MXC note sequences. This handles insertions and
deletions without cascading positional mismatches.

### Pitched-only similarity

The primary metric. Compares only pitched notes (excludes rests) between
prediction and reference using sequence alignment.

A sample with many rest measures followed by a few notes would score 0% on
rests-included metrics even if the notes are correct. Pitched-only avoids this.

### Other metrics

| Metric | What it measures |
|---|---|
| Pitched-only similarity | Correct notes in correct order (excl. rests) |
| Positional accuracy | % of notes correct at each position (strict) |
| Rhythm similarity | Note types/durations correct |
| Combined similarity | Pitch + type together |
| Note coverage | Predicted events / reference events |
| Longest match | Longest consecutive correct run |
| Header accuracy | Key, time signature, part structure |

### Interpreting results

- **< 10%**: Model not reading pitch from image
- **10-30%**: Some pitch learning, with alignment drift
- **30-50%**: Meaningful pitch reading, room to improve
- **> 50%**: Good accuracy (check longest match for consistency)
- **Coverage > 100%**: Model hallucinating extra notes

## Usage

```bash
# Evaluate latest WandB run
python eval_mxc.py --latest

# Specific run with trend across steps
python eval_mxc.py --wandb-run wandb/run-XXXXX --by-step

# Compare two MXC files directly
python eval_mxc.py --pred prediction.mxc --ref reference.mxc
```

## Caveats

- Metrics are computed on 10 held-out samples during training, not the full
  dev set (193 samples). Results may not be statistically robust.
- `SequenceMatcher` finds the longest common subsequence, which can match
  notes that happen to share a pitch but are in different measures.
- The metric does not distinguish between a wrong pitch and a missing note
  followed by a correct one — both reduce the similarity score.
- Lyric order (L1 vs L2) sometimes differs between prediction and reference
  without affecting musical content.

## Implementation

- **Script**: `vlm_omr_sft/eval_mxc.py`
- **Guide**: `vlm_omr_sft/reports/evaluation-guide.md`
markdown.srcMarkdownNoYaml:
## Metrics

We use alignment-aware sequence matching (`SequenceMatcher`) to compare
predicted and reference MXC note sequences. This handles insertions and
deletions without cascading positional mismatches.

### Pitched-only similarity

The primary metric. Compares only pitched notes (excludes rests) between
prediction and reference using sequence alignment.

A sample with many rest measures followed by a few notes would score 0% on
rests-included metrics even if the notes are correct. Pitched-only avoids this.

### Other metrics

| Metric | What it measures |
|---|---|
| Pitched-only similarity | Correct notes in correct order (excl. rests) |
| Positional accuracy | % of notes correct at each position (strict) |
| Rhythm similarity | Note types/durations correct |
| Combined similarity | Pitch + type together |
| Note coverage | Predicted events / reference events |
| Longest match | Longest consecutive correct run |
| Header accuracy | Key, time signature, part structure |

### Interpreting results

- **< 10%**: Model not reading pitch from image
- **10-30%**: Some pitch learning, with alignment drift
- **30-50%**: Meaningful pitch reading, room to improve
- **> 50%**: Good accuracy (check longest match for consistency)
- **Coverage > 100%**: Model hallucinating extra notes

## Usage

```bash
# Evaluate latest WandB run
python eval_mxc.py --latest

# Specific run with trend across steps
python eval_mxc.py --wandb-run wandb/run-XXXXX --by-step

# Compare two MXC files directly
python eval_mxc.py --pred prediction.mxc --ref reference.mxc
```

## Caveats

- Metrics are computed on 10 held-out samples during training, not the full
  dev set (193 samples). Results may not be statistically robust.
- `SequenceMatcher` finds the longest common subsequence, which can match
  notes that happen to share a pitch but are in different measures.
- The metric does not distinguish between a wrong pitch and a missing note
  followed by a correct one — both reduce the similarity score.
- Lyric order (L1 vs L2) sometimes differs between prediction and reference
  without affecting musical content.

## Implementation

- **Script**: `vlm_omr_sft/eval_mxc.py`
- **Guide**: `vlm_omr_sft/reports/evaluation-guide.md`
